mission.name: openclaw-architect-convergence
mission.objective: Continuously converge all target SaaS repos on canonical core patterns derived from MCP-indexed exemplars and prevent drift.

branding_rules: No user-facing 'NirvanaPass' strings; product name must be 'CookiesPass' or 'CookiesPass Tempe'.
Location can remain 'Cookies Tempe' or similar where it refers to the store, not the pass product.
No user-facing 'NirvanaPass' strings; product name must be 'CookiesPass' or 'CookiesPass Phoenix'.
Location 'Nirvana West Phoenix' is allowed only where it describes the dispensary, not the pass product.

phases.name: day1_mcp_scan_and_spec
phases.description: Use MCP over CookiesPass/cookies-tempe + TempeCookiesPass/nirvaan and Stripe exemplar repos (BlackWallStreetopoly, v0-skyn-patch) to map pricing/routes/auth/email and lock the CookiesPass v1 spec in docs/COOKIESPASS-V1-SPEC.md. No new UI or core implementation yet.
done_when: Spec file docs/COOKIESPASS-V1-SPEC.md exists and covers createCheckoutSession, handleStripeWebhook, sendEmail contracts.
MCP sweeps confirm no raw stripe.* calls in local/CookiesPass, local/cookies-tempe, local/TempeCookiesPass, or nirvaan/CookiesPass.
MCP sweeps confirm no user-visible 'NirvanaPass' branding remains in local/TempeCookiesPass or nirvaan/CookiesPass.

phases.name: day2_implement_core_flows
phases.description: Implement core-backed CookiesPass flows: fill in core/stripe and core/email using exemplar patterns, and add thin /api/checkout + /api/stripe/webhook routes in both TempeCookiesPass (Vercel) and nirvaan/CookiesPass (Replit) that call the cores only.
done_when: core/stripe.createCheckoutSession and core/stripe.handleStripeWebhook are implemented and covered by basic tests.
TempeCookiesPass exposes /api/checkout and /api/stripe/webhook routes that never touch the Stripe SDK directly and use metadata.source='cookiespass'.
nirvaan/CookiesPass exposes /api/checkout and /api/stripe/webhook routes that never touch the Stripe SDK directly and use metadata.source='cookiespass'.

phases.name: day3_polish_and_demo_hardening
phases.description: Polish UX, run focused MCP landmine/drift sweeps for all CookiesPass variants, and harden logging so the CookiesPass demo can show events flowing end-to-end on both Vercel and Replit.
done_when: MCP sweeps on local/CookiesPass, local/cookies-tempe, local/TempeCookiesPass, and nirvaan/CookiesPass show no new landmines (raw stripe., execSync in request paths, JSON state anti-patterns).
Happy-path CookiesPass checkout works end-to-end on both Vercel and Replit, with receipt and welcome emails sent via core/email and logs visible for demo on each host.

external_skills.description: Office/PDF workflows (DOCX redlining, PDF conversion) and MCP-builder helpers.
recommended_use: Use as canonical patterns for doc/PDF processing skills.
Borrow validation and redlining helpers for report and contract automation.
Reference when designing MCP-backed tools that operate on Office/PDF documents.

external_skills.description: SWE lifecycle debugging and reasoning skills, including graph/diagram rendering.
recommended_use: Use for systematic debugging flows when modernizing Trigger.dev tasks, PM2 scripts, or Stripe/email/queue code.
Use graph/diagram helpers to visualize complex flows for docs and reports.

external_skills.description: Session catch-up and persistent planning context across large codebases.
recommended_use: Default planning skill for multi-day refactors (PayClaw, ClawPay, CookiesPass).
Run before large missions to generate a structured summary of current state and next steps.

external_skills.description: Meta-skill for generating and auditing skill prompts and SKILL definitions.
recommended_use: Use as an internal "skill factory" when designing new OpenClaw/ClawPay/PayClaw skills.
Apply prompt-extractor/xray helpers to audit existing skills and agent prompt files.

external_skills.description: Catalog of 140+ specialized subagent roles and workflows.
recommended_use: Use as a role catalog when expanding config/agent-team.json or designing new mission agents.
Do not vendor code; treat as an idea/reference source for agent responsibilities.
